data.title: Elon Musk Supports Telegram Founder in Criticism of EU and UK Content Moderation
data.description: Telegram founder Pavel Durov has criticized EU and UK authorities, accusing them of leveraging child safety concerns to negotiate private content moderation agreements with social media executives. According to NS3.AI, Elon Musk has expressed support for Durov's stance. On the same day, French prosecutors requested Musk's presence for a voluntary interview regarding allegations that X, his platform, enabled the distribution of child abuse material and deepfakes. Durov also mentioned that his own legal issues in France are still under investigation.

data.title: Texas Oil Pipelines Strain Under High Demand Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Texas oil pipelines are operating beyond their intended capacity due to a surge in international demand for U.S. crude. Bloomberg posted on X that this increased demand is a result of disruptions in oil shipments caused by the ongoing conflict involving Iran. The situation has led to a significant strain on the infrastructure, as global markets seek alternatives to the stranded cargoes. The heightened activity underscores the critical role of U.S. oil in stabilizing global energy supplies amid geopolitical tensions.

data.title: Bitcoin climbs toward $77,000 as risk sentiment improves, but altcoins remain under pressure following $290M KelpDAO exploit
data.description: Key takeaways›Bitcoin rose to $76,500 and briefly spiked to $77,000 before meeting resistance near Friday's high of $78,300.›Ether and DeFi tokens continue to lag after the weekend's $290 million KelpDAO exploit, with sentiment in the sector still fragile.›Easing Middle East tensions could lower oil prices and provide a further tailwind for risk assets including crypto.›Bitcoin and ether funding rates remain negative, setting up a potential short squeeze if price resilience holds.Crypto markets are showing tentative signs of recovery on Tuesday, led by bitcoin reclaiming the $76,500 level as broader risk sentiment improves — though the altcoin market remains firmly in the shadow of the weekend's major DeFi security breach.Bitcoin pushes higher, faces key resistanceBitcoin advanced roughly 1% from midnight UTC, reaching $76,500 before a brief spike to approximately $77,000 at 9:45 a.m. The move was quickly met by a wave of spot sellers, widely seen as market participants defending a potential breakout above Friday's swing high of $78,300 — a level that, if cleared, could open the door to further upside.U.S. stock index futures also rose on the day, pointing to a broader return of risk-on appetite among investors. The crypto market's correlation with equities sentiment has remained strong in recent weeks, and Tuesday's equity strength appears to be providing a tailwind for bitcoin even as geopolitical uncertainty lingers.Altcoins struggle in the wake of the KelpDAO exploitEther significantly underperformed bitcoin, adding just 0.3% as investors maintained a cautious stance toward altcoins in the aftermath of the $290 million exploit on KelpDAO over the weekend. DeFi tokens bore the brunt of the fallout, with ethena (ENA), etherfi (ETHFI) and jupiter (JUP) all posting losses over the past 24 hours despite a marginal recovery since midnight.AAVE, which shed 22% over the weekend in the immediate aftermath of the exploit, managed to claw back 2.6% on Tuesday — though the recovery comes against a backdrop of broadly negative sentiment across the DeFi sector. Open interest in AAVE futures has climbed to a record 3.59 million tokens, but the OI-adjusted cumulative volume delta has turned negative, suggesting sell orders are dominating — pointing to a slight bearish bias despite the price recovery.Asset24h PerformanceOI ChangeBitcoin (BTC)+1–3%↑Solana (SOL)+1–3%↑BNB+1–3%↑Ether (ETH)+0.3%↓ slightDogecoin (DOGE)Negative↓ slightENA / ETHFI / JUPNegative↓AAVE+2.6%↑ (record OI)CoinMarketCap's Altcoin Season indicator sits at 39 out of 100, recovering from a weekend low of 34 but still firmly signaling investor preference for bitcoin over altcoins. The CoinDesk Memecoin Index is the worst-performing benchmark of the day, down 0.24%, while the bitcoin-dominant CoinDesk 20 is up 0.65%. The broader CoinDesk 80 altcoin index remained essentially flat through both the Asia and European trading sessions.Derivatives: short squeeze potential buildsThe derivatives market is painting a cautious but nuanced picture. The long-short ratio for crypto futures sits at 50.68%, reflecting a near-even split between bullish and bearish positioning — a sign that traders are broadly undecided on the market's next directional move.Bitcoin and ether funding rates have turned negative, indicating a bias toward short positions in the perpetual futures market. While bearish on the surface, this environment also creates the conditions for a potential short squeeze — a scenario in which sustained price resilience forces short sellers to close their positions, adding further momentum to any upward move in spot prices.On the CME, activity in BTC futures continues to cool even as spot bitcoin ETFs continue to attract inflows. This divergence suggests that ETF buyers are taking straightforward directional long positions rather than engaging in basis trades — a signal of genuine bullish conviction rather than arbitrage activity. On Deribit, BTC and ETH put options continue to trade at a premium to calls, reflecting lingering downside concerns among options market participants. Bitcoin straddles and strangles — volatility plays that profit from large moves in either direction — account for more than half of all block flow activity over the past 24 hours.Geopolitics: Middle East developments in focusBeyond crypto-specific catalysts, macro and geopolitical developments remain a key driver of market direction. The ongoing conflict involving Iran continues to weigh on risk sentiment, with crypto and oil prices showing an inverse correlation since hostilities began. The U.S. vice president is due to travel to Pakistan for peace talks, and any signs of de-escalation could push oil prices lower — potentially providing a meaningful boost to risk assets including crypto.For now, markets appear to be cautiously optimistic, with bitcoin holding firm above key support levels and broader risk appetite showing tentative signs of recovery. Whether that momentum can be sustained will depend heavily on how geopolitical developments unfold — and whether the DeFi sector can rebuild confidence in the wake of one of the year's largest exploits.

data.title: AI Gear Exports from South Korea and Taiwan Remain Resilient Amid Mideast Tensions
data.description:
Exports of artificial intelligence (AI) equipment from South Korea and Taiwan are showing resilience despite the ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. Bloomberg posted on X that these two Asian economies continue to maintain robust export levels of AI-related products, even as regional conflicts pose challenges to global trade dynamics.

The Middle East, a significant market for technology exports, has been experiencing heightened instability, which typically affects international trade flows. However, South Korea and Taiwan have managed to sustain their export activities, indicating strong demand for AI technologies.

Industry analysts suggest that the sustained export levels are driven by the increasing global reliance on AI technologies across various sectors, including finance, healthcare, and manufacturing. This demand is helping to buffer the potential negative impacts of geopolitical uncertainties on trade.

Both South Korea and Taiwan are key players in the global technology supply chain, with advanced capabilities in producing semiconductors and other critical components for AI systems. Their ability to continue exporting AI gear underscores the strategic importance of these technologies in the current global economic landscape.

As the situation in the Middle East evolves, the resilience of AI gear exports from these countries will be closely monitored by industry stakeholders and policymakers.

data.title: Toncoin Rises 6% Amid Whale Buying and Scalability Improvements
data.description: Toncoin experienced a 6% increase in value over the past 24 hours, emerging as the top performer among the 100 largest cryptocurrency projects by market capitalization. According to NS3.AI, a report from BSCN highlighted significant whale activity, with 189,730 TON purchased over the last three months. Telegram founder Pavel Durov announced plans to enhance the network's scalability and reduce transaction fees by up to six times.

data.title: Iran Prepares for Potential Conflict as Ceasefire Deadline Approaches
data.description:
On April 21, Iran is bracing for a possible return to conflict as the ceasefire deadline nears its end. According to BlockBeats, negotiations have stalled due to demands from the United States and a maritime blockade, leading Tehran to anticipate a resumption of hostilities.

Recent reports indicate that Iran has spent weeks redeploying military forces and updating its target list. Officials have stated that they are ready to launch immediate and intensified strikes against U.S. and Israeli forces should the conflict reignite.

data.title: Helius CEO Discusses Decentralization in Blockchain
data.description:
Helius CEO Mert shared insights on decentralization via the X platform, emphasizing that decentralization fundamentally means coordination without a central authority. According to ChainCatcher, Mert explained that if coordination costs are nearly zero, the system is effectively centralized; conversely, if costs are infinitely high, the system cannot function properly and thus cannot be considered decentralized.

Mert illustrated this concept with a thought experiment involving a highly decentralized blockchain with hundreds of thousands of home-operated nodes, distributed mining power, and token supply. He posed a hypothetical scenario where North Korea could transfer funds from Wallet A to Wallet B within a specific timeframe, equating this capability to the chain's potential to simultaneously trigger nuclear weapons globally.

He further speculated on whether a blockchain could be considered decentralized if it successfully forked to freeze Wallet A's funds without causing mass casualties. Mert concluded that while humans might coordinate due to the shared incentive of avoiding death, this does not imply the chain is unorganized. Decentralization remains defined by coordination without central authority.

data.title: Bitcoin Faces Resistance Amid Market Dynamics
data.description: Bitcoin is encountering resistance at the $78,000 and $83,000 levels, according to Schwab digital assets strategist Jim Ferraioli. According to NS3.AI, these resistance points are influenced by active investor and spot Bitcoin ETP cost-basis levels. Ferraioli highlighted the CLARITY Act as a significant factor that could potentially shift the momentum in the cryptocurrency market. Additionally, CoinShares data revealed that there were $1.5 billion in U.S.-led inflows last week, indicating strong investor interest.

data.title: Vitol Group Reports Strong Performance Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Vitol Group's Chief Executive Officer, Russell Hardy, has stated that the company's performance has been 'good' this year despite the challenges faced during the early days of the Iran war. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Hardy's remarks on the company's resilience in navigating the complexities of the geopolitical situation. The energy trading firm has managed to maintain stability and growth, showcasing its ability to adapt to volatile market conditions. Hardy's comments reflect confidence in Vitol Group's strategic approach and operational capabilities during turbulent times.

data.title: European Financial Entities Urge EU to Expedite DLT Pilot Review
data.description:
Nasdaq, Boerse Stuttgart Group, and 39 other signatories from fintech associations in France, Spain, Italy, Hungary, and Poland have called on the European Commission and the European Parliament to expedite the review of the 'Distributed Ledger Technology (DLT) Pilot Regime' by separating it from the broader 'Market Integration and Regulation Program.' According to Foresight News, the signatories argue that the lengthy negotiation process of the overall program poses a risk of Europe falling behind the United States in DLT applications.

In a letter to Financial Services Commissioner Maria Luis Albuquerque, the signatories requested the removal of asset class restrictions, an increase in the overall transaction volume cap to 150 billion euros, and the elimination of license time limits. The letter highlighted that the United States established a regulatory framework for stablecoins through the 'Genius Act' last year, rapidly gaining a leading position in the tokenized assets sector. The European Commission is currently pushing for a swift resolution of the entire regulatory program, viewing it as a crucial part of the Capital Markets Union plan. Financial Services Commissioner Maria Luis Albuquerque has previously emphasized the need for all legislative proposals to be passed simultaneously.

data.title: Liquid Capital Founder Reflects on Crypto Industry Insights Since 2015
data.description:
Liquid Capital founder Yi Lihua shared his experiences since entering the crypto industry in 2015. According to Odaily, he emphasized the importance of strategic direction, timing, and risk management in the sector's development.

Yi highlighted the significance of choosing the right niche, such as exchanges, and noted that entering the market during a bear phase can help build long-term confidence. He advised against engaging in high-risk contract trading and stressed the importance of selecting reliable partners, maintaining physical and mental health, and continuously learning new technologies and opportunities.

data.title: BNB Surpasses 640 USDT with a 2.10% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 21, 2026, 10:47 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 640 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 640.119995 USDT, with a narrowed 2.10% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Lambda256 Partners with Crystal Intelligence for Digital Asset Compliance in South Korea
data.description: Lambda256 has entered into a business agreement with Crystal Intelligence to provide an integrated digital asset compliance solution in South Korea. According to NS3.AI, this collaboration aims to address the anti-money laundering and counter-terrorism financing requirements associated with digital assets. The partnership is expected to enhance regulatory compliance and security measures within the digital asset sector.

data.title: Yi Lihua: The key to the crypto industry lies in direction, timing, and risk management.
data.description: Liquid Capital founder Yi Lihua summarized his experience since entering the crypto industry in 2015, emphasizing the importance of focusing on direction selection, entry timing, and risk management. He pointed out that choosing the right sector (such as exchanges) is crucial, and entering during a bear market helps build long-term confidence. He also stressed avoiding high-risk contract trading, paying attention to partner selection, maintaining mental and physical health, and continuously learning about new technologies and opportunities.

data.title: 3M Shares Rise 2% Pre-Market as Q1 Adjusted EPS Exceeds Expectations
data.description: 3M's stock saw a 2% increase in pre-market trading following the announcement of its first-quarter adjusted earnings per share (EPS) surpassing market expectations. According to Jin10, the company's financial performance in the first quarter has been stronger than anticipated, contributing to the positive movement in its stock price.

data.title: General Electric Exceeds Profit Expectations Amid Strong Air Travel Demand
data.description: General Electric reported first-quarter profits that surpassed Wall Street forecasts, driven by robust demand for air travel. Bloomberg posted on X that the jet-engine manufacturer managed to navigate disruptions linked to the ongoing conflict in Iran. The company's performance highlights resilience in the aviation sector despite geopolitical challenges. GE's ability to meet market expectations underscores the strength of its operations and the continued recovery in global travel.

data.title: AMD Develops MRM-Based CPO Solution for Next-Gen Instinct MI500 Accelerator
data.description: AMD announced plans to develop a Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) solution based on MRM technology for its upcoming Instinct MI500 accelerator. According to Jin10, the Photon Integrated Circuit (PIC) will be manufactured by GlobalFoundries, while ASE Technology Holding Co., Ltd. will handle the packaging process. This development marks a significant step in enhancing the performance and efficiency of AMD's accelerator technology.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Health Insurance Companies See Pre-Market Gains Following UnitedHealth's Strong Q1 Profit
data.description: U.S. health insurance companies experienced a rise in pre-market trading after UnitedHealth Group (UNH.N) reported first-quarter profits that exceeded expectations. According to Jin10, CVS Health shares increased by 3.3%, Elevance Health saw a 3.2% rise, Cigna's stock went up by 1.3%, Humana gained 2.9%, and Molina Healthcare rose by 3%. The positive earnings report from UnitedHealth appears to have bolstered investor confidence across the sector.
